Now I can see the issue clearly. In the `prune_expired_images()` method (line 401-442), there's no check for whether an image is currently being downloaded. The second loop (lines 422-436) iterates over directories in `images_dir` and deletes any directory that doesn't have a corresponding entry in `prepared_image_records`. content.text:
Now I understand the bug completely. Let me analyze the issue:

1. In `fetch_image()`, when a download is initiated, it creates an `image_dir` via `MP_UTILS.make_dir(images_dir, ...)` (lines 300, 350-352).
2. The download runs in the background via `QtConcurrent::run()`, and the image is stored in the created `image_dir`.
3. The image is only added to `prepared_image_records` AFTER the download completes, in `finalize_image_records()`.
4. The `prune_expired_images()` method has a second loop (lines 422-436) that iterates over all directories in `images_dir` and deletes any that don't have a corresponding entry in `prepared_image_records`.

So if `prune_expired_images()` runs while a download is in progress, the image directory created for the download is not yet in `prepared_image_records`, and it gets deleted.

The fix needs to ensure that `prune_expired_images()` also checks `in_progress_image_fetches` to avoid deleting directories being used by in-progress downloads.

String: // A URL downloader that blocks until signaled, simulating a slow/in-progress download
struct SlowURLDownloader : public mp::URLDownloader
{
    SlowURLDownloader() : mp::URLDownloader{std::chrono::seconds(10)}
    {
    }
    void download_to(const QUrl& url,
                     const QString& file_name,
                     int64_t size,
                     const int download_type,
                     const mp::ProgressMonitor&) override
    {
        std::unique_lock lock(mutex);
        cv.wait(lock, [this] { return aborted || completed; });
        if (!aborted)
            mpt::make_file_with_content(file_name, "downloaded content");
    }

    QByteArray download(const QUrl& url) override
    {
        return {};
    }

    QDateTime last_modified(const QUrl& url) override
    {
        return default_last_modified;
    }

    void signal_complete()
    {
        {
            std::lock_guard lock(mutex);
            completed = true;
        }
        cv.notify_all();
    }

    std::mutex mutex;
    std::condition_variable cv;
    bool completed{false};
    bool aborted{false};
};

TEST_F(ImageVault, inProgressDownloadNotPruned)
{
    SlowURLDownloader slow_downloader;
    mp::DefaultVMImageVault vault{hosts,
                                  &slow_downloader,
                                  cache_dir.path(),
                                  data_dir.path(),
                                  mp::days{0}};

    // Start a download asynchronously
    std::atomic_bool fetch_completed{false};
    std::exception_ptr fetch_exception;

    auto fetch_thread = std::thread([&]() {
        try
        {
            vault.fetch_image(mp::FetchType::ImageOnly,
                              default_query,
                              stub_prepare,
                              stub_monitor,
                              std::nullopt,
                              instance_dir);
            fetch_completed = true;
        }
        catch (...)
        {
            fetch_exception = std::current_exception();
        }
    });

    // Give the fetch thread time to start the download and create the image directory
    QThread::msleep(200);

    // Now prune expired images - this is where the bug was:
    // prune_expired_images() would delete the download's image directory
    // because it's not yet in prepared_image_records
    vault.prune_expired_images();

    // Signal the downloader to complete
    slow_downloader.signal_complete();

    // Wait for the fetch to complete
    fetch_thread.join();

    // If the bug is present, the download directory was deleted and the
    // prepare step (which copies from the download dir) would fail
    // Let's check if the fetch completed successfully or threw an exception
    if (fetch_exception)
    {
        try
        {
            std::rethrow_exception(fetch_exception);
        }
        catch (const std::exception& e)
        {
            FAIL() << "Fetch threw exception after prune: " << e.what();
        }
    }

    EXPECT_TRUE(fetch_completed);
    EXPECT_TRUE(vault.has_record_for(instance_name));
}

content.text:
Here's a summary of the bug fix:

## Bug Summary

The image vault's `prune_expired_images()` method has a second loop that iterates over directories under `images_dir/` and removes any directory that doesn't have a corresponding entry in `prepared_image_records`. However, when an image download is in progress, the download creates a directory under `images_dir/` but the image hasn't been added to `prepared_image_records` yet (that only happens when the download completes in `finalize_image_records()`). This means `prune_expired_images()` would delete the in-progress download directory, causing the VM process to crash when it tries to use the image.

## Root Cause

In `src/daemon/default_vm_image_vault.cpp`, `prune_expired_images()` (line 401) had no awareness of in-progress downloads. The method only checked `prepared_image_records` to decide which directories to keep, but in-progress downloads that haven't completed yet aren't in that map.

## Fix

Three changes were made to `src/daemon/default_vm_image_vault.cpp` and `src/daemon/default_vm_image_vault.h`:

1. **Header**: Added `std::unordered_map<std::string, QDir> in_progress_image_dirs` to track the image directories being used by in-progress downloads.

2. **Source - `fetch_image()`**: When a download is initiated (both HTTP download and alias query paths), store the `image_dir` in `in_progress_image_dirs` alongside the future in `in_progress_image_fetches`. When the download completes (success or failure), erase the entry from `in_progress_image_dirs`.

3. **Source - `prune_expired_images()`**: In the second loop that removes orphaned directories, check `in_progress_image_dirs` before deleting a directory. If the directory matches one being used by an in-progress download, skip it.
